content.problemStatement:
Joseph is captured along with **N**-1 of his soldier friends. The enemy has decided to play the following game.

These **N** soldiers are seated in a circle. The seats are numbered from 1 to **N** in clockwise order. The enemy chooses a nonzero integer **K**.


If **K** is positive the enemy kills the **K**th guy in clockwise direction starting from 1 (the 1st guy starting from 1 is 1 itself). Then the (2**K**)th alive guy in anticlockwise direction from the position of first killing, then (3**K**)th alive guy in clockwise direction from position of second killing and so on until only one guy survives.

Similarly, if **K** is negative the enemy kills the |**K**|th guy in anticlockwise direction starting from 1. Then the (|2**K**|)th guy in clockwise direction from the position of first killing, then (|3**K**|)th guy in anticlockwise direction from the position of second killing and so on until only one guy survives.

That is, after every killing the enemy reverses its direction and increases the number of soldiers skipped before next killing by **K**.

Note that the starting direction is clockwise if **K**>0 and anticlockwise if **K**<0 and the counting for the next killing starts from the position of previous killing.

You have to determine the index at which Joseph should sit in order to survive.
constraints: Sum of N over all testcases is less than or equal to 10^6 for each input file.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each of the following T lines contain two integers each: N and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer in a new line: the index where Joseph must choose to survive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first test case is the example given in the problem statement. The second test case is also an example given in the problem statement.
The people are seated in a circle and the enemy kills the 3rd person in the clockwise direction. Then the enemy kills the 6th person in the anticlockwise direction, and so on.
The people are seated in a circle and the enemy kills the 2nd person in the clockwise direction. Then the enemy kills the 4th person in the anticlockwise direction, and so on.

solution.approach: The problem is a variation of the Josephus problem. We can solve it using dynamic programming. The idea is to simulate the game and keep track of the position of the last person to survive. We can use a queue to keep track of the people and their positions.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — explain: per query is O(N), total is O(T * N)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — explain what dominates: the queue to keep track of people

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
def josephus(n, k):
    if n == 1: return 1
    if k > 0: return (josephus(n - 1, k) + k - 1) % n + 1
    else: return (josephus(n - 1, -k) - (-k) - 1 + n) % n + 1

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n, k = map(int, input().split())
    print(josephus(n, k))
